seoTitle: Fitzroy Island vs Flagstone Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fitzroy Island and Flagstone Creek. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Fitzroy Island vs Flagstone Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fitzroy Island and Flagstone Creek.
heading: Fitzroy Island vs Flagstone Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fitzroy Island, Queensland and Flagstone Creek, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fitzroy Island has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Flagstone Creek (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fitzroy Island or Flagstone Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fitzroy Island has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Flagstone Creek scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fitzroy Island scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Flagstone Creek scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fitzroy Island or Flagstone Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fitzroy Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
